text: Passing --model-think or --model-work without --model-mode auto should fail loudly (non-zero exit, clear message), not be silently accepted and ignored. Today scaffold-workflow.cjs exits 0 and the flags are no-ops in default mode, which hides operator mistakes. A check should assert that `--model-think fable` WITHOUT `--model-mode auto` exits non-zero.

In --model-mode auto, every think-class agent() call (Brief, RED, PathGap, CodeGate, Review, TestGate, DocsGate, Writeup) should also carry effort: 'high'; mechanical and work phases must NOT get an effort opt. Today auto emits a model: opt but never an effort: opt (grep of a generated --profile delivery --model-mode auto script shows zero `effort:` occurrences).

The generator's stdout summary should report the active model mode so the operator sees it at a glance. Today scaffold-workflow.cjs prints the phase order and launch-readiness but never states whether --model-mode is default or auto (nor the resolved think/work model picks). A check should assert that running with `--model-mode auto` prints a line mentioning the mode.

Flag-validation errors should echo the offending value to speed debugging. Today `--model-mode bogus` prints `ERROR: --model-mode must be 'default' or 'auto'` without showing `bogus`, and `--model-think gpt` lists the allowed set without showing `gpt`. A check should assert the error output for an invalid value contains that value.
